#  Filing Options
[Home](https://dor.mo.gov/) » [Taxation ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation) » [Individual ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual) » [Tax Types ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types) » [Property Tax Credit ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types/property-tax-credit) » [Filing Options ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types/property-tax-credit/filing-options.html)
Individuals claiming the Property Tax Credit have these options for filing their claims: 
  * E-File ([see below](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types/property-tax-credit/filing-options.html#efile)) 
  * 2D Barcode, available through qualified tax preparers 
  * Short form (paper) 
    * [MO-PTC](https://dor.mo.gov/forms/?formName=mo-ptc&category=&year=&searchForms=Search+Forms) - Use this form if you are: 
      * Not required to file an individual income tax return; 
      * Not filing a federal income tax return; and 
      * Claiming a property tax credit only. 
  * Long form (paper) 
    * [MO-1040](https://dor.mo.gov/forms/?formName=MO-1040&category=&year=&searchForms=Search+Forms) with MO-PTS (Form MO-PTS is included in the Form MO-1040 file) - Use these forms if you are: 
      * Required to file an individual income tax return; and 
      * Have positive or negative modifications (eg. railroad retirement); or 
      * Claiming a miscellaneous tax credit; or 
      * Have recapture tax or tax on a lump sum distribution. 
    * [Forms you can complete on your computer.](https://dor.mo.gov/forms/?category=7)


The Department of Revenue wants to ensure you use the easiest form for your filing situation. If you are unsure which form best meets your filing needs, use our [Tax Form Selector](https://mytax.mo.gov/rptp/portal/home/tax-form-selector). The Department of Revenue also offers [free tax assistance](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/tax-information-office.html). 
##  E-File with your Tax Preparer 
You may ask your tax preparer to e-file your federal and Missouri returns, provided he or she is an authorized Electronic Return Originator (ERO), or you can check the [Internal Revenue Service's list of ERO's](https://www.irs.gov/). If you are eligible for a Property Tax Credit and required to file federal and Missouri (Form MO-1040) income tax forms, you are eligible to e-file.

#  Pension Exemption & Social Security Disability Deduction
[Home](https://dor.mo.gov/) » [Taxation ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation) » [Individual ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual) » [Tax Types ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types) » [Property Tax Credit ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types/property-tax-credit) » [Pension ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types/property-tax-credit/pension.html)
##  How do I determine if I qualify for an exemption? 
  * [Qualification Chart**PDF Document**](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/individual/tax-types/property-tax-credit/documents/pension.pdf)


**You may use Form MO-1040 and Form MO-A.** (Attach Form MO-PTS to claim the property tax credit.) See the notes below for more help! 
**Use Form MO-1040 and Form MO-A if you are claiming a pension and social security/social security disability exemption and you:**
  * Have income from another state; or 
  * Have positive or negative modifications to federal adjusted gross income; or 
  * Are claiming miscellaneous tax credits; or 
  * Have recapture tax or tax on a lump sum distribution. 


##  Still confused or have more questions? 
The department wants to ensure you use the easiest form for your tax filing situation. If you are unsure which form best meets your filing needs, use our [Tax Form Selector](https://mytax.mo.gov/rptp/portal/home/tax-form-selector). 
##  Pension Exemption Forms 
  * [Form MO-PTS](https://dor.mo.gov/forms/?formName=MO-PTS&category=&year=&searchForms=Search+Forms)
  * [Form MO-1040](https://dor.mo.gov/forms/?formName=MO-1040&category=&year=&searchForms=Search+Forms)
  * [Form MO-A](https://dor.mo.gov/forms/?formName=MO-A&category=&year=&searchForms=Search+Forms)

#  Tax Assistance
[Home](https://dor.mo.gov/) » [Taxation ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation) » [Tax Information Office ](https://dor.mo.gov/taxation/tax-information-office.html)
There are many ways to get assistance regarding your taxes.
## DORA
DORA is our virtual assistant and can be accessed 24/7 to help answer any questions you have. Click on DORA in the lower right-hand corner of your screen to get started.
## [MyTax Portal](https://mytax.mo.gov/rptp/portal/home/!ut/p/z1/04_Sj9CPykssy0xPLMnMz0vMAfIjo8zijS0MnN09DIy83EODjQwc_R1DTcIcvYAsE_1wQgqigNIGOICjAVB_FCElBbkRBumOiooA7YFcLQ!!/dz/d5/L2dBISEvZ0FBIS9nQSEh/)
Business Tax Customers are encouraged to use the MyTax Portal. This service allows you to register an account where you can file and pay returns and track the returns and payments for Sales Tax, Use Tax, Employer Withholding Tax and Tire and Lead-Acid Battery Fee. Other services such as requesting a No Tax Due Certificate or requesting a payment plan are also available.
Individual Tax Customers can also register an account. You may also file a Missouri Property Tax Credit Claim, request a payment plan or inquire about the status of your income tax return.
## Tax Information Office
A Tax Information Office is open in the Harry S Truman State Office Building, 301 W. High Street, Room 330, Jefferson City MO 65101. Hours are 8:00am-4:45pm.
Customers can submit their payments and documents using a secure drop-box located within the office. Customers can also ask questions regarding their accounts or notices received. Tax return preparation is NOT available, but may be available from a [**volunteer program in your area**](https://www.irs.gov/individuals/free-tax-return-preparation-for-you-by-volunteers).
[**Driving directions to the Tax Information Office**](http://maps.google.com/maps?f=d&source=s_d&saddr=&daddr=301+West+High+St.,+Jefferson+City,+MO+65101&hl=en&geocode=&mra=ls&sll=38.578864,-92.175046&sspn=0.011004,0.01929&ie=UTF8&z=16)
To contact the Department by phone or email [find the correct phone number or address](https://dor.mo.gov/contact/).
